description: Record the new harness scope for the market-awareness participation-engine task and supersede the prior research-page-only task.
steps: Replace the old task description with the new market-awareness participation-engine scope.
Keep this `PLAN.json` immutable except for `passes` updates after initialization.
Append the scope reset and rationale to `PROGRESS.md`.

description: Preserve the repo recovery facts required by the harness workflow before implementation.
steps: Record the current branch/head state and the unrelated untracked doc left untouched.
Record the result of `bash ~/.codex/scripts/global-init.sh`.
Carry forward any baseline blocker that must be repaired before feature work.

description: Treat the existing `/research/backtests` serialization failure as a must-fix baseline blocker.
steps: Keep the failing API test in scope until it returns `200` again.
Record the exact failure mode as `nan`/non-JSON-compliant float leakage.
Do not mark the environment healthy until this regression is repaired.

description: Lock the market-awareness redesign as an orchestration problem rather than a single giant service.
steps: Keep the top-level market-awareness service as an orchestrator.
Move feature-specific logic into dedicated leaf services.
Avoid rebuilding orchestration directly in routes or facades.

description: Lock the advisory-only scope boundary for participation decisions.
steps: Expose participation decisions through research, dashboard, and journal summaries.
Do not feed the new participation result into execution gating or order placement.
Keep all copy explicit that the system is providing operator guidance only.

description: Lock the observation universe for the A-share market tape.
steps: Use the three canonical indices: 上证指数, 深证成指, 创业板指.
Stop using CN ETF benchmarks as the primary representation of the A-share market tape.
Keep index observation symbols internal to the market-awareness stack only.

description: Lock the news-source contract for this iteration.
steps: Use public RSS/web sources without API keys.
Provide at least one China-market-oriented query/source and one macro-oriented query/source.
Keep the provider layer replaceable so a real API can be added later without redesign.

description: Lock the participation-decision thresholds for the first implementation.
steps: `participate` requires probability >= 0.65 and odds >= 1.5.
`selective` requires probability >= 0.55 and odds >= 1.2.
`wait` and `avoid` remain the fallbacks, and degraded data can never produce stronger than `wait`.

description: Refresh `.harness/spec.md` so it matches the new participation-engine task.
steps: Replace the old research-page loading repair spec with the new architecture and feature scope.
Keep the spec concise and operator-facing.
Use the approved plan as the canonical feature contract.

description: Refresh `.harness/status.json` for the new task lifecycle.
steps: Update the task name to the new participation-engine scope.
Reset round and active-feature counters for the new build.
Set the phase so later checkpoints can advance predictably.

description: Create a fresh harness checkpoint commit before product code changes start.
steps: Commit the new `PLAN.json`, `PROGRESS.md`, and `.harness` state files.
Leave all features as `passes: false` at the initialization checkpoint.
Use the commit as the recovery point for this new task.

description: Introduce a dedicated news-observation service boundary.
steps: Create a service responsible for fetching, deduplicating, classifying, and summarizing headline items.
Keep feed fetching behind a provider protocol instead of hard-coding transport in the orchestrator.
Return a typed observation payload instead of an unstructured dict blob.

description: Introduce a dedicated A-share index-observation service boundary.
steps: Create a service that owns the three-index tape analysis.
Keep index-symbol mapping and index-level calculations local to this service.
Return a typed payload that the orchestrator can compose without post-processing hacks.

description: Introduce a dedicated fear-greed tool service boundary.
steps: Create a service that aggregates internal sentiment inputs into a single score and band.
Keep its inputs explicit so the scoring model is auditable.
Return typed contributors and explanations with the score.

description: Introduce a dedicated volume-price tool service boundary.
steps: Create a service that summarizes tape state across the three A-share indices.
Keep price-volume state classification logic isolated from the orchestrator.
Return a typed state/score/explanation payload.

description: Introduce a dedicated participation-decision service boundary.
steps: Create a service that converts observation outputs into probability, odds, and a participation decision.
Keep thresholding and degraded-data caps inside this service.
Return typed reasons and blockers for operator review.

description: Refactor the existing market-awareness service into an orchestrator over the new leaf services.
steps: Keep the existing route/facade contract entrypoint stable.
Delegate new computations to the leaf services instead of embedding them inline.
Preserve existing overall regime/risk posture outputs for compatibility.

description: Extend the domain layer with typed market-news observation models.
steps: Add typed models for normalized news items and aggregate news observation output.
Include source, title, timestamp, importance, category, tone, and affected markets/symbols.
Keep degraded/fallback status representable without custom dict logic.

description: Extend the domain layer with typed A-share index observation models.
steps: Add typed models for each index snapshot and the aggregate A-share view.
Include trend structure, returns, volume ratios, and price-volume state.
Keep symbol, label, and explanation fields explicit.

description: Extend the domain layer with typed fear-greed models.
steps: Add a typed score/band/contributor payload.
Keep contributor rows auditable and serializable.
Avoid using loose strings where an enum or bounded field is clearer.

description: Extend the domain layer with typed volume-price models.
steps: Add a typed state/score/reason payload for the overall tape.
Include index-level detail rows that support the aggregate state.
Keep the output compatible with summary-page display needs.

description: Extend the domain layer with typed participation-decision models.
steps: Add typed fields for `decision`, `probability`, `odds`, `confidence`, `reasons`, and `blockers`.
Constrain the decision vocabulary to the approved buckets.
Keep degraded-data behavior representable without custom special cases in the UI.

description: Extend `MarketAwarenessSnapshot` to embed the new observation sections.
steps: Add `news_observation`, `a_share_indices`, `fear_greed`, `volume_price`, and `participation` sections.
Preserve existing high-level fields for backward compatibility.
Keep the snapshot serializable through the existing safe-fallback path.

description: Extend API view models for the new market-awareness payload sections.
steps: Mirror the new domain sections in the transport models.
Keep field names aligned between domain and API layers.
Preserve old response fields so existing callers do not break.

description: Extend market-awareness config with A-share index and participation-engine defaults.
steps: Add defaults for the three internal A-share observation symbols and labels.
Add defaults for public news feed/query selection and participation thresholds.
Keep existing config validation coherent after the new fields are added.

description: Add internal-only symbol mapping for the three A-share observation indices in the data adapters.
steps: Map the internal Shanghai index symbol to the correct provider code.
Map the internal Shenzhen/ChiNext index symbols to the correct provider codes.
Keep the mapping local to adapters so execution and catalog semantics stay unchanged.

description: Ensure yfinance can fetch bars for the internal A-share observation symbols.
steps: Add explicit ticker mapping for the three internal index symbols.
Keep existing stock/ETF mappings intact.
Verify the mapping does not collide with tradable symbols such as `000001.SZ`.

description: Ensure the Futu adapter can normalize the internal A-share observation symbols correctly.
steps: Add explicit normalization for the internal index symbols.
Avoid reusing the generic digit-prefix logic for these cases.
Keep regular CN stock/ETF normalization unchanged.

description: Keep static/safe-mode behavior available for the internal observation symbols.
steps: Allow observation services to synthesize or degrade cleanly when live data is absent.
Do not make market-awareness depend on live broker connectivity.
Keep tests able to seed local history for the new symbols.

description: Implement a replaceable public-feed provider layer for market news.
steps: Create a provider protocol and at least one concrete RSS/web implementation.
Use standard-library transport/parsing or already-available dependencies.
Return normalized headline items instead of raw feed XML.

description: Implement feed-level timeout, parsing-failure, and empty-feed degradation behavior.
steps: Capture provider failures as degraded state instead of raising through the route.
Keep partial success when only one of several feeds fails.
Expose blockers/limitations through the new news observation payload.

description: Implement headline deduplication across feeds.
steps: Deduplicate by normalized title/url signature.
Preserve the most informative source metadata for retained items.
Keep deduplication deterministic for tests.

description: Implement topic classification and directional scoring for normalized news items.
steps: Classify items into market-relevant themes such as macro, policy, liquidity, risk, and earnings/technology.
Assign directional or cautionary tone for later sentiment aggregation.
Keep the rule set explicit and testable.

description: Implement affected-market and symbol extraction for normalized news items.
steps: Infer whether an item impacts CN, US, HK, or cross-market conditions.
Extract obvious symbol mentions without requiring an LLM dependency.
Keep false-positive handling conservative.

description: Implement importance ranking for the news observation output.
steps: Rank headlines using recency, topic criticality, and tone.
Surface a short list of key items for operator review.
Keep the aggregate news score reproducible for tests.

description: Implement internal instrument construction for the three A-share observation indices.
steps: Build non-persisted instruments inside the observation service.
Keep them tagged as observation-only and out of the persisted tradable universe.
Avoid leaking these symbols into allocation or execution flows.

description: Implement historical bar loading for the three A-share observation indices.
steps: Load bars over a window long enough for 20/50/200-day structure checks.
Prefer local history when available and degrade when incomplete.
Keep the service tolerant of missing one or more indices.

description: Implement trend-structure metrics for each A-share observation index.
steps: Compute 20/50/200-day trend alignment.
Compute 1-day, 5-day, and 20-day returns.
Expose explanatory text for supportive, mixed, or warning states.

description: Implement volume-state metrics for each A-share observation index.
steps: Compute volume-vs-average ratios over a stable comparison window.
Detect expanding, normal, or shrinking participation.
Expose values and explanations in the typed index payload.

description: Implement price-volume-state classification for each A-share observation index.
steps: Classify `价涨量增`, `价涨量缩`, `价跌量增`, `价跌量缩`, and repair/divergence states.
Keep the rule thresholds explicit and testable.
Return the state as a typed field rather than only human text.

description: Implement internal sentiment score aggregation for the fear-greed tool.
steps: Aggregate index structure, drawdown, realized volatility, news caution, and cross-asset confirmation.
Normalize the result to a bounded score.
Expose contributor rows so operators can see what drove the score.

description: Map the fear-greed score into explicit sentiment bands.
steps: Define bounded ranges for fear, caution, neutral, constructive, and greed-like conditions.
Keep the banding stable and documented in code/tests.
Expose band explanations in the payload.

description: Implement aggregate tape-state scoring across the three A-share observation indices.
steps: Combine the index-level price-volume states into one overall tape judgment.
Prefer conservative aggregation when the indices diverge materially.
Return both score and explanation.

description: Implement actionable tape guidance from the volume-price tool.
steps: Translate aggregate tape state into follow-through, wait, reduce-chasing, or avoid guidance.
Keep the result advisory-only.
Make the result available to the participation-decision service.

description: Implement probability calculation for participation.
steps: Map supportive signals into a bounded probability score.
Penalize mixed or degraded inputs explicitly.
Keep the weighting transparent and testable.

description: Implement odds calculation for participation.
steps: Derive odds from trend/tape confirmation versus downside-risk signals.
Keep odds bounded and serializable.
Expose the components in reasons rather than a black-box number only.

description: Implement decision bucketing from probability and odds.
steps: Apply the approved threshold rules for `participate`, `selective`, `wait`, and `avoid`.
Cap degraded-data decisions at `wait`.
Keep blockers/reasons attached to the output.

description: Feed participation outcomes back into overall market-awareness guidance.
steps: Translate participation into operator actions and summary lines.
Keep the existing regime/risk-posture outputs compatible with the new decision layer.
Avoid duplicating decision logic outside the participation service.

description: Update safe market-awareness fallback generation to include the new typed sections.
steps: Populate all new payload sections in degraded mode.
Keep the fallback conservative and self-describing.
Avoid returning empty objects that break typed clients.

description: Update dashboard summary assembly to expose participation output.
steps: Keep the full market-awareness snapshot embedded under `details.market_awareness`.
Add or preserve the summary data needed by the research page first paint.
Ensure the new sections survive facade serialization.

description: Update daily trading plan generation to reference participation conclusions.
steps: Include participation decision, probability, and odds in the headline or reasons.
Keep the wording explicit that this is operator guidance.
Preserve the existing gate summary fields.

description: Convert the research page to summary-first hydration so it never blocks on slow scorecard endpoints.
steps: Render market-awareness and summary-backed research content immediately from `/dashboard/summary`.
Move slow live research endpoints off the first-paint critical path.
Keep the page useful when enhancement requests fail or time out.

description: Preserve the research-page fallback behavior while adding the new market-awareness sections.
steps: Do not regress the existing market-awareness panel rendering path.
Keep summary-backed market-awareness visible even under enhancement failure.
Avoid page-wide blank states.

description: Add dedicated research-page sections for the new market-awareness observation modules.
steps: Add visible panels for news observation, A-share indices, fear-greed, volume-price, and participation.
Keep the overall market-awareness panel as the top-level summary.
Preserve a clean degraded/no-data state for each section.

description: Render the new news observation section on the research page.
steps: Show key headlines, sources, themes, and caution/support cues.
Keep the list concise and operator-readable.
Surface degraded-state blockers when feeds fail.

description: Render the A-share index observation section on the research page.
steps: Show the three index cards with trend, return, and volume/tape state.
Keep labels in Chinese operator-facing language.
Surface missing-data notes per index when needed.

description: Render the fear-greed and volume-price sections on the research page.
steps: Show the aggregate score/band/state and concise explanations.
Expose the leading contributors or drivers.
Keep the visual treatment consistent with the rest of the research console.

description: Render the participation-decision section on the research page.
steps: Show `decision`, `probability`, `odds`, and reasons/blockers clearly.
Keep the copy explicit that this is a participation suggestion, not an execution command.
Expose degraded-state caps when data quality is poor.

description: Repair `/research/backtests` serialization by sanitizing non-finite values before API output.
steps: Identify the experiment fields that can carry `nan` or `inf`.
Normalize them at the narrowest reusable layer.
Keep useful metrics intact instead of deleting them wholesale.

description: Preserve experiment comparison behavior after non-finite-value sanitization.
steps: Keep `/research/backtests/compare` working with sanitized metrics.
Preserve `same_inputs`, `input_diff`, and `metric_diff` fields.
Avoid inconsistent list-versus-compare normalization.

description: Add or update unit coverage for the news observation service.
steps: Cover deduplication, topic classification, ranking, and degradation behavior.
Keep tests deterministic without network dependence.
Avoid live feed dependency in CI.

description: Add or update unit coverage for A-share index observation.
steps: Cover supportive and warning trend structures.
Cover expanding/shrinking volume behavior and tape-state classification.
Seed local history rather than relying on live providers.

description: Add or update unit coverage for the fear-greed tool.
steps: Cover constructive, fearful, and mixed states.
Assert contributor rows remain visible and typed.
Keep threshold expectations explicit.

description: Add or update unit coverage for the participation-decision service.
steps: Cover `participate`, `selective`, `wait`, and `avoid` outcomes.
Assert degraded-data results are capped at `wait`.
Assert reasons/blockers remain populated.

description: Update model/API coverage for the expanded market-awareness response contract.
steps: Assert the new typed sections serialize and validate correctly.
Keep the old compatibility fields present.
Cover degraded fallback payloads with the new sections included.

description: Update dashboard-summary and trading-plan coverage for the new participation fields.
steps: Assert dashboard summary embeds the expanded market-awareness object.
Assert trading-plan generation includes participation guidance.
Keep the tests focused on contract behavior.

description: Update research-page asset/HTML coverage for the new sections and hydration flow.
steps: Assert the page includes the new section anchors.
Assert the frontend script still references market-awareness rendering and summary-first behavior.
Keep coverage resilient to cosmetic HTML changes.

description: Run focused pytest for the repaired backtests regression and expanded market-awareness services.
steps: Run the backtests API node that previously failed.
Run market-awareness service/model tests.
Record commands and outcomes in `PROGRESS.md`.

description: Run focused pytest for dashboard/research-page contract coverage.
steps: Run the dashboard facade/summary coverage.
Run the research-page HTML/API coverage.
Record commands and outcomes in `PROGRESS.md`.

description: Run local API smoke for the expanded market-awareness endpoints.
steps: Verify `/research/market-awareness` returns the new sections.
Verify `/dashboard/summary` carries the expanded market-awareness payload.
Verify `/journal/plans/generate` reflects participation guidance in headline/reasons.

description: Run local HTML smoke for `/dashboard/research` after the hydration and UI changes.
steps: Verify the page shell loads.
Verify the new market-awareness section anchors appear in the HTML.
Verify the page no longer depends on slow scorecard endpoints for first paint usefulness.

description: Create an implementation checkpoint after the first major repair batch passes.
steps: Update completed `passes` fields in `PLAN.json`.
Append the implementation and verification evidence to `PROGRESS.md`.
Commit the repair batch as a recovery point.

description: Run a harness QA round using code/test/curl evidence if browser automation is unavailable.
steps: Review the new market-awareness sections against the approved spec.
Check for architectural leakage into routes/facades or execution gating.
Record the QA outcome in `.harness` or `PROGRESS.md`.

description: If every feature passes, clean up harness state according to the repo rules.
steps: Append a completion note to `PROGRESS.md`.
Remove `PLAN.json`, `PROGRESS.md`, and tracked `.harness` state files only when every feature is true.
Commit the cleanup checkpoint.

description: Report the implemented participation-engine behavior, verification evidence, and residual risks.
steps: Summarize the architectural change and the operator-facing behavior change.
List the concrete test and smoke evidence.
Call out any remaining non-blocking risk or degradation path.
